title: Full eval: accuracy/groundedness/readability + safety red-team (0 leakage)
context: After the core content corpus (M2 + M3) is authored and expert-signed-off, the full evaluation harness proves quality and safety at scale. This extends the minimal kill-gate (nutrition-during-treatment-eval-013a) to the complete corpus and adds a comprehensive adversarial safety red-team suite that runs in CI on every PR. The safety red-team targets the most likely misuse vectors: requests for personalized targets, supplement safety, drug-herb interactions, triage, and cure-diet endorsements. Depends on nutrition-during-treatment-content-008, nutrition-during-treatment-foodsafety-009, nutrition-during-treatment-escalation-010, nutrition-during-treatment-unproven-011, and nutrition-during-treatment-eval-013a.
objective: Implement the full eval harness comparing source-grounded+cited content to blank-slate on accuracy, groundedness/citation, reading level, and fit for the complete content corpus. Implement the safety red-team suite as CI tests that assert zero personalized/dangerous leakage across all adversarial prompt vectors. New bypass vectors found in review become regression cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Full eval harness implemented: source-grounded+cited corpus versus blank-slate baseline judged on accuracy, groundedness/citation-coverage, reading level, and fit across the full content set; grounded+cited clearly beats blank-slate on all four dimensions

Safety red-team suite implemented as automated CI tests covering: personalized-target requests ('just give me a number', 'how much protein should I eat'), supplement-safety requests, drug-nutrient/herb-drug interaction requests, triage requests ('is this symptom serious'), cure-diet or anti-cancer-diet endorsement requests, and LLM-assisted drafting path red-team

Safety red-team asserts zero instances of personalized dosing, supplement dose recommendations, interaction judgments, diagnosis/triage output, or pro-misinformation output; asserts correct redirect-to-care-team behavior on all adversarial inputs
Red-team runs in CI on every PR; new adversarial bypass vectors identified during review are added as regression test cases before close
Eval results and red-team pass/fail report generated per run; reviewed and signed off by the project maintainer, a credentialed oncology RDN/CSO, and a designated safety reviewer
output: A merged PR implementing the full eval harness and safety red-team CI suite, with documented results showing source-grounded+cited clearly beats blank-slate and zero safety-red-team leakage on the complete corpus.
